items.title: CMS Finalizes Rule to Simplify Payer-Provider Disputes Under No Surprises Act - MedCity News
items.reason: This item reports on CMS finalizing a rule directly related to No Surprises Act implementation, specifically addressing the Independent Dispute Resolution (IDR) process for payer-provider disputes, which is a core component of the topic definition.
items.summary: CMS has finalized a rule to streamline the payer-provider dispute resolution process under the No Surprises Act. The rule simplifies procedures for resolving disagreements between payers and providers regarding out-of-network billing and balance-billing claims under the IDR framework. This regulatory update directly impacts how healthcare providers and payers manage claims disputes and compliance with No Surprises Act protections. Revenue-cycle teams must understand the procedural changes to adjust dispute submission processes, documentation requirements, and timelines for IDR cases.
